commands.review_summary:
The PR changes ACP CLI option parsing so `--no-prefix-cwd` maps to `prefixCwd: false` and adds focused ACP CLI tests for opt-out and default behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main plus Commander 14.0.3 source shows the flag writes `prefixCwd`, while the current action reads `noPrefixCwd`; I did not execute the repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the code change is already focused and appears correct; the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof or a maintainer proof override.

target.title: fix(cli): honor --no-prefix-cwd in acp
target.body:
## Summary

- Let Commander own the negated option default for .
- Read the positive  option that Commander actually populates.
- Add ACP CLI coverage for both the explicit opt-out and the default behavior.

Closes #83901.

## Verification

- 
- Crabbox AWS , run : regression failed before the source patch, then the focused ACP CLI and translator tests passed after the patch ().

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:  now forwards  into the ACP bridge so prompts are not prefixed with ; the default path still forwards .
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux lease , run , using a clean remote clone.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: 
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with ; the same regression test failed before the source patch with  received as .
Observed result after fix:  dispatches the ACP bridge with ; omitted flag dispatches with ; translator prompt-prefix behavior remains covered.
What was not tested: no long-running interactive ACP server was started; the CLI option contract and translator output behavior were verified through focused tests.
